summary: Red-team review of the 3LS Authority Boundary Firewall + preflight repair suggestions. Seven adversarial scenarios exercised: TLC leak, PQX leak, dashboard/API coverage, fake allowlist attempt, missing neutral vocabulary, scan failure, and S4+blocking=false bypass attempt. One S3 finding (dashboard scope gap) recorded and resolved with a registry scope_note plus a tractable future scope-extension path. No S2+ findings. Existing CI authority leak guard remains strict and unweakened (11 existing tests pass).

findings.description: TLC non-owner authority leak (decision/allow) is caught by the preflight and a neutral-vocabulary suggestion is emitted.
PQX non-owner authority leak (decision/block) is caught by the preflight and a neutral-vocabulary suggestion is emitted.

Dashboard/API surface (dashboard/, app/, components/) is outside default_scope_prefixes in authority_registry.json. Authority vocabulary leaks under those paths are not currently caught by the existing CI guard or by the new preflight.
findings.recommendation: DASHBOARD recorded in three_letter_system_authority with empty owner_path_prefixes and explicit scope_note. Future scope-extension PR can add dashboard scan paths without breaking existing usage. See FIX-001.

findings.description: Fake allowlist override attempt: the repair suggester never proposes adding a non-owner path to vocabulary_overrides. Owner-path violations are flagged for manual owner review instead of silent override widening.
Missing neutral vocabulary map fails closed: ThreeLetterAuthorityPreflightError is raised and the preflight exits non-zero.
Scan failure (malformed JSON / broken Python) is wrapped in ThreeLetterAuthorityPreflightError with file context. Preflight exits non-zero.
findings.recommendation: No action. Same fail-closed pattern as scripts/run_authority_leak_guard.py.

findings.description: S4 + blocking=false bypass attempt: review-artifact metadata cannot mask authority shape. Vocabulary and shape detectors operate on artifact content, not severity tags.
Existing CI authority leak guard is not weakened. All 11 existing authority-leak tests pass after the registry extension.
findings.recommendation: No action. Verified by existing test suite plus tests/governance/test_3ls_authority_preflight.py::test_existing_authority_leak_guard_still_catches_forbidden_value.
